literature_map:
ThiyageswaranKokotBrennanMeilaYuFazel2026 formulates worst-case HT-GATE design as an SDP over X = E[ZZ'] and writes the trade-off objective explicitly in Eq. (1.1) as a linear Laplacian-plus-pseudoinverse criterion plus a robustness norm penalty, then certifies only one-sided performance through Gaussian rounding.
ChenLiDengWang2024 gives the closest pre-anchor covariance-design reduction and states the central feasibility obstruction: an optimized covariance matrix need not come from any legitimate assignment law.
HuberMaric2017 identifies the exact realizable correlation set for symmetric Bernoulli margins with the cut polytope, making the implementability obstruction geometric rather than only algorithmic.
BandeiraKunisky2018 clarifies how the elliptope strictly relaxes cut-matrix structure and why extreme-point exactness is special rather than generic.
LaurentMascarinTelen2026 studies graph-elliptope boundary geometry and is the right source for the idea that a linear objective can expose non-cut boundary points even on low-dimensional graph slices.
VivianoLeiImbensKarrerSchrijversShi2026 gives the causal-design consumer: finite-sample worst-case MSE chooses graph-aware cluster structure, but it does not study when the relaxed covariance optimum is exactly implementable.
HarshawSavjeSpielmanZhang2024 provides the robustness-vs-balance benchmark for implementable randomized designs; it motivates the Bernoulli corner of the phase diagram.
ChatterjeeDeyGoswami2023 sharpens the implementable Gram-Schmidt benchmark by identifying the precise limiting variance, which is the natural downstream comparison for any rounding-loss certificate.
RaghavendraTan2012 shows that global treatment-count constraints fundamentally change SDP exactness and rounding, so the balanced-cardinality extension is a separate problem rather than a routine appendix.
The live gap is therefore objective-level: no cited paper gives a family-specific exactness region, a cut-break threshold in eta/gamma, or a positive implementability-gap certificate for the interference-homophily SDP itself.
seeds: Two-block homophily phase diagram: exact cut-attainment for small eta/gamma, exact Bernoulli attainment for large kappa, and a strict implementability-gap window in between.
Closed-form cut-break threshold on the balanced two-block mean graph where the community-sign design ceases to solve the elliptope relaxation.
One-sided-sharp rounding-loss certificate rho*(G, eta/gamma, kappa) for replacing the SDP optimum by the best implementable +-1 design.
Necessary-and-sufficient characterization of the block-exchangeable +-1 covariance slice inside the two-block elliptope via block-sum moment laws.
Robustness-dominated exactness theorem: large-kappa balanced Bernoulli randomization exactly solves the SDP on a named homophily family.
Balanced-cardinality extension: exactness and gap boundary under fixed treatment counts rather than unconstrained Rademacher laws.
Cycle-completable graph angle: objective-exposed non-cut elliptope boundary as the source of positive implementability loss.
Finite-m LP certificate for implementability on the block-symmetric slice, turning the abstract cut obstruction into an experimentally checkable feasibility test.

seed_details.one_line: Two-block homophily phase diagram: exact cut-attainment for small eta/gamma, exact Bernoulli attainment for large kappa, and a strict implementability-gap window in between.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Characterize, for balanced two-community homophily graphs, exactly when the interference-design SDP optimum is attainable as the second-moment matrix of a randomized +-1 assignment.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a dressed-up max-cut relaxation statement on a toy SBM graph.
seed_details.rebuttal: The causal object is not max-cut but the worst-case HT-GATE MSE design objective with an L^dagger term and a robustness penalty, and the deliverable is implementability of randomized assignments rather than partition recovery.

seed_details.one_line: Closed-form cut-break threshold on the balanced two-block mean graph where the community-sign design ceases to solve the elliptope relaxation.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Derive the family-specific eta/gamma critical threshold at which a cut-aligned implementable design stops attaining the SDP optimum and a positive +-1 implementability gap begins.
seed_details.referee_objection: The threshold could collapse to a renamed optimizer value with no independent mathematical content.
seed_details.rebuttal: The viable version ties the threshold to dual support of the block-symmetric elliptope slice and compares it against the block-exchangeable implementable cone, so it is a frontier in primitives rather than a tautological value function.
seed_details.status: survived as the main open frontier inside the chosen angle

seed_details.one_line: One-sided-sharp rounding-loss certificate rho*(G, eta/gamma, kappa) for replacing the SDP optimum by the best implementable +-1 design.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Produce a tight or one-sided-sharp rounding-loss certificate for the price of replacing the elliptope SDP design by an implementable randomized +-1 design.
seed_details.referee_objection: A rho* bound without a zero-loss exactness map is just another approximation-ratio paper.
seed_details.rebuttal: That objection is right, so this survives only as part of the phase-diagram angle where rho*=0 on the exactness cells and positive in the transition window.

seed_details.one_line: Necessary-and-sufficient characterization of the block-exchangeable +-1 covariance slice inside the two-block elliptope via block-sum moment laws.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Characterize, for balanced two-community homophily graphs, exactly when the interference-design SDP optimum is attainable as the second-moment matrix of a randomized +-1 assignment.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a polyhedral subproblem with weak causal content on its own.
seed_details.rebuttal: Agreed; it matters only as the engine behind the causal design phase boundary, not as the standalone paper.

seed_details.one_line: Robustness-dominated exactness theorem: large-kappa balanced Bernoulli randomization exactly solves the SDP on a named homophily family.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Identify the robustness-dominated kappa regime in which balanced Bernoulli or spread-out randomization exactly attains the relaxation optimum, and the neighboring regime where robustness creates positive implementability loss.
seed_details.referee_objection: Large-kappa exactness alone is just a corner case of the anchor's robustness discussion.
seed_details.rebuttal: By itself yes, which is why it is retained only as one corner theorem inside the chosen phase-diagram kernel.
seed_details.status: survived as one theorem in the chosen angle

seed_details.one_line: Balanced-cardinality extension: exactness and gap boundary under fixed treatment counts rather than unconstrained Rademacher laws.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Extend the +-1 exactness and rounding-loss boundary from unconstrained Rademacher assignment laws to fixed-treatment-fraction or globally balanced +-1 designs.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is really a second paper because global cardinality constraints change the feasible covariance polytope.
seed_details.rebuttal: The objection lands; the fixed-count extension is a clean follow-on only after the unconstrained block-exchangeable boundary is understood.

seed_details.one_line: Cycle-completable graph angle: objective-exposed non-cut elliptope boundary as the source of positive implementability loss.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Characterize exactness failure as exposure of non-cut elliptope boundary points, not merely generic elliptope membership.
seed_details.referee_objection: The causal-design consumer gets too indirect once the graph family is no longer tied to a homophily design benchmark.
seed_details.rebuttal: The objection lands for v1; the graph-elliptope geometry is retained as method inspiration rather than as the headline family.

seed_details.one_line: Finite-m LP certificate for implementability on the block-symmetric slice, turning the abstract cut obstruction into an experimentally checkable feasibility test.
seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Produce a finite feasible-object certificate showing when the relaxed covariance can or cannot be realized by one shared assignment law.
seed_details.referee_objection: This risks repeating the prior-bank failure where a feasibility certificate becomes the whole contribution.
seed_details.rebuttal: Exactly; it is useful only as a supporting lemma inside the broader phase-structure paper, not as the kernel itself.

novelty_justification.repo_axis: The closest in-catalogue analogue is exp_saturation_skew_threshold_v1, which pursued an exact design threshold in a one-dimensional moment program but failed when the claimed phase variable vanished under the working model. The current angle avoids that defect by making the shared covariance object itself the theorem target and by importing the pid_network_exposure and pid_network_random_set lesson that feasibility must be for one common latent assignment law, not cellwise or marginally. No in-repo proposal studies elliptope-versus-implementable exactness for assignment covariances under interference.
novelty_justification.published_axis: ThiyageswaranKokotBrennanMeilaYuFazel2026 is the direct comparator: it solves the relaxed covariance SDP and rounds, but it does not characterize when rounding is unnecessary or when a strict implementability loss is inevitable. ChenLiDengWang2024 explicitly states the feasibility obstruction for optimized covariances but does not derive a graph-family exactness frontier. HuberMaric2017 characterizes realizable symmetric Bernoulli correlations abstractly via the cut polytope, but it does not connect that feasible set to a causal design objective involving L, L^dagger, and robustness. The proposal's gap is therefore the family-specific phase boundary linking those ingredients.

symbols.name: m
symbols.def: Number of units in each of the two equal-sized communities.

symbols.name: n
symbols.def: Total number of units, n = 2m.

symbols.name: a
symbols.def: Normalized within-community edge weight in the two-block homophily mean graph.

symbols.name: b
symbols.def: Normalized cross-community edge weight in the two-block homophily mean graph.

symbols.name: A_m, B_m
symbols.def: The two communities, each of cardinality m, forming a partition of the n units.

symbols.name: 1_n
symbols.def: The n-vector of ones.

symbols.name: I_n
symbols.def: The n x n identity matrix.

symbols.name: J_n
symbols.def: The rank-one matrix J_n = 1_n 1_n'.

symbols.name: s_m
symbols.def: The vector taking value 1 on A_m and -1 on B_m.

symbols.name: G_m(a,b)
symbols.def: The balanced two-community homophily mean graph with weights a/m within blocks and b/m across blocks.

symbols.name: L_m
symbols.def: The Laplacian matrix of G_m(a,b).

symbols.name: L_m^dagger
symbols.def: The Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse of L_m.

symbols.name: Z
symbols.def: The realized binary treatment vector encoded in plus-minus-one form.

symbols.name: Y_i(z)
symbols.def: The fixed potential outcome of unit i under assignment vector z in {-1,1}^n.

symbols.name: P
symbols.def: A randomized experimental design law for Z.

symbols.name: X(P)
symbols.def: The assignment covariance proxy X(P) = E_P[ Z Z' ].

symbols.name: tau
symbols.def: The finite-population global average treatment effect comparing the all-treated and all-control assignments.

symbols.name: r
symbols.def: For nonnegative objective weights eta,gamma with gamma>0, the homophily-to-interference ratio r = eta/gamma; hence r>=0 is definitional, not a separate proof hypothesis.

symbols.name: kappa
symbols.def: The weight on the Schatten-2 robustness penalty in the normalized design objective.

symbols.name: X(u,v)
symbols.def: The matrix with diagonal 1, common within-block off-diagonal entry u, and common across-block entry v.

symbols.name: E_m^blk
symbols.def: The set of block-symmetric correlation matrices X(u,v) that are positive semidefinite.

symbols.name: P_m^bal
symbols.def: Admissible plus-minus-one assignment laws with global sign symmetry; this implies zero unit-level first moments.

symbols.name: P_m^sym
symbols.def: Laws in P_m^bal that are invariant under permutations within A_m and within B_m.

symbols.name: C_m^pm
symbols.def: The set { X(P) : P in P_m^sym } of block-exchangeable realizable plus-minus-one second moments.

symbols.name: F_{r,kappa}(X)
symbols.def: F_{r,kappa}(X) = Tr(L_m X) + r Tr(L_m^dagger X) + kappa ||X||_{S_2} + Tr(J_n X).

symbols.name: P_cut
symbols.def: The law assigning probability 1/2 to s_m and 1/2 to -s_m.

symbols.name: X_cut
symbols.def: The covariance matrix X_cut = X(P_cut) = s_m s_m'.

symbols.name: P_iid
symbols.def: The independent Rademacher design with P_iid(Z_i=1)=P_iid(Z_i=-1)=1/2.

symbols.name: Delta_m^pm(r,kappa)
symbols.def: Delta_m^pm(r,kappa) = inf_{P in P_m^sym} F_{r,kappa}(X(P)) - inf_{X in E_m^blk} F_{r,kappa}(X).

symbols.name: kappa_cut(m,a,b)
symbols.def: The largest robustness weight below which a nontrivial cut-exactness region can persist on the block-symmetric slice.

symbols.name: r_cut(m,a,b,kappa)
symbols.def: For 0 <= kappa < kappa_cut(m,a,b), the largest ratio value up to which the cut covariance remains optimal on the block-symmetric elliptope slice.

symbols.name: r_gap^-(m,a,b,kappa)
symbols.def: For 0 <= kappa < kappa_cut(m,a,b), the lower and upper ratio frontiers delimiting a proven positive implementability-gap window.

symbols.name: r_gap^+(m,a,b,kappa)
symbols.def: For 0 <= kappa < kappa_cut(m,a,b), the lower and upper ratio frontiers delimiting a proven positive implementability-gap window.

symbols.name: rho_star(m,a,b,r,kappa)
symbols.def: The tight causal-MSE price of restricting the SDP optimum to implementable plus-minus-one designs.

symbols.name: B(P)
symbols.def: The joint law of the two community sums (sum_{i in A_m} Z_i, sum_{i in B_m} Z_i) under P.

novel.justification: This family is the deterministic mean graph of the balanced two-community SBM and the canonical finite-population benchmark for homophily-driven cluster design.

assumptions.condition: P(Z = z) = P(Z = -z) for all z in {-1,1}^n.

novel.justification: This proposal-specific restriction keeps the admissible design class centered at half-treatment margins while still containing the main implementable candidates of interest, including the iid Rademacher law and the symmetric two-point cut law P_cut.
novel: finite-family scale normalization for the chosen objective with unit balance penalty
standard: parity feasibility condition for sums of m Rademacher signs

definitions.name: G_m(a,b)
definitions.construction: Vertex set A_m union B_m with |A_m|=|B_m|=m; for i != j in the same block set W_ij = a/m, for i and j in opposite blocks set W_ij = b/m, and set W_ii = 0.

definitions.name: P_m^bal
definitions.construction: { P on {-1,1}^n : P(Z=z)=P(Z=-z) for every z }

definitions.name: P_m^sym
definitions.construction: { P in P_m^bal : P is invariant under permutations within A_m and within B_m }

definitions.name: F_{r,kappa}(X)
definitions.construction: Tr(L_m X) + r Tr(L_m^dagger X) + kappa ||X||_{S_2} + Tr(J_n X)

definitions.name: Delta_m^pm(r,kappa)
definitions.construction: inf_{P in P_m^sym} F_{r,kappa}(X(P)) - inf_{X in E_m^blk} F_{r,kappa}(X)

definitions.name: B(P)
definitions.construction: Law of (sum_{i in A_m} Z_i, sum_{i in B_m} Z_i) under P.

statements.statement: Assume ass:two-block-homophily, with r in [0,infty) and kappa in [0,infty) by their definitional domains. Orbit-averaging over the full two-block automorphism group generated by permutations within A_m, permutations within B_m, and the swap of the two equal communities maps each elliptope point X with diag(X)=1 to a point in E_m^blk with weakly smaller F_{r,kappa}. The same averaging maps each law P in P_m^bal to a block-exchangeable law with the same zero one-point margins and weakly smaller F_{r,kappa}(X(P)). Consequently inf_{X : X is positive semidefinite and diag(X)=1} F_{r,kappa}(X) = inf_{X in E_m^blk} F_{r,kappa}(X) and inf_{P in P_m^bal} F_{r,kappa}(X(P)) = inf_{P in P_m^sym} F_{r,kappa}(X(P)).
statements.justification: The proposal only becomes sharp once the geometry reduces to a two-parameter covariance slice rather than the full n x n elliptope.
statements.gap: ThiyageswaranKokotBrennanMeilaYuFazel2026 and ChenLiDengWang2024 optimize over X but do not prove a family-specific exact symmetrization for implementable laws and relaxed covariances simultaneously.
statements.consumer: Every downstream threshold and gap statement can then be expressed in the block parameters (u,v) instead of an intractable ambient covariance.

statements.statement: Assume ass:two-block-homophily. Set r_cut(m,a,b,kappa)=min{2b(a+b)(1-kappa/(a-b)), 2b(2m-2b-kappa)}. If 0<=r<r_cut(m,a,b,kappa), then X_cut is the unique minimizer of F_{r,kappa} over E_m^blk. Hence Delta_m^pm(r,kappa)=0, X_cut is the unique minimizer over C_m^pm, and P_cut attains that implementable optimum. (The range 0<=r<r_cut(m,a,b,kappa) is nonempty exactly when 0<=kappa<min{a-b,2(m-b)}, which in particular requires b<m; this is a non-vacuity remark, not a hypothesis.) At the boundary r=r_cut uniqueness can fail already when kappa=0, so the theorem uses the strict inequality r<r_cut.
statements.justification: This is the sharp pure-cut corner promised by the topic: the SDP optimum is not merely rounded well but exactly realized by a feasible randomized design.
statements.gap: ThiyageswaranKokotBrennanMeilaYuFazel2026 gives Gaussian rounding but no zero-loss exactness theorem, and VivianoLeiImbensKarrerSchrijversShi2026 identifies cut-like causal cluster designs without a covariance-implementability boundary.
statements.consumer: Experimenters on homophilous two-block networks can certify when community-aligned randomization already attains the relaxed optimum, so no rounding or walk-based substitute is needed.

statements.statement: Assume ass:two-block-homophily. Finite-kappa iid exactness holds only on the affine-balanced locus. More precisely, I_n is a minimizer of F_{r,kappa} over E_m^blk for some finite kappa if and only if a+3b=2m and r=2b(a+b), equivalently r=4b(m-b). On this locus, for every kappa>0, I_n is the unique relaxed minimizer and the implementable optimum is attained by P_iid, with P_iid in P_m^sym by def:block-exchangeable-design-class and X(P_iid)=I_n. Off this locus, I_n is not a minimizer for any finite kappa, although minimizers converge to I_n as kappa tends to infinity.
statements.justification: A genuine phase diagram needs the opposite exact corner as well, not just the cut side.
statements.gap: ThiyageswaranKokotBrennanMeilaYuFazel2026 Proposition 7 shows that increasing the robustness penalty drives the relaxed solution toward Bernoulli randomization, but it does not give a finite family-specific threshold at which the iid covariance becomes exactly optimal.
statements.consumer: This gives a rigorous certificate for when robustness concerns dominate community alignment, making the simplest spread-out randomized design optimal.

statements.statement: Assume ass:two-block-homophily, ass:low-scale-two-block, and ass:odd-community-size. With q=2(m-1), define kappa_gap(m,a,b):=((2m-a-3b)(a-b)sqrt(q))/(a+b) and r_cut_gap(m,a,b,kappa):=2b(a+b)(1-kappa/(a-b)). Define R_x^-(m,a,b,kappa)=2b(a+b)(1+kappa/((a-b)sqrt(q))) and R_x^+(m,a,b,kappa)=(a+b)(2m-a-b-kappa/sqrt(q)). Define r_gap^-(m,a,b,kappa)=(R_x^-(m,a,b,kappa)+R_x^+(m,a,b,kappa))/2 and r_gap^+(m,a,b,kappa)=R_x^+(m,a,b,kappa). Then for every 0 <= kappa < kappa_gap(m,a,b), r_cut_gap(m,a,b,kappa) < r_gap^-(m,a,b,kappa) < r_gap^+(m,a,b,kappa), and for every r in (r_gap^-,r_gap^+) the spread vertex X_spread in E_m^blk with spectral coordinates (m/(m-1),0,0) is the unique relaxed minimizer but is not plus-minus-one implementable. Consequently Delta_m^pm(r,kappa)>0 throughout that interval. The oddness restriction is necessary: if m is even, the whole block elliptope slice is implementable and the claimed positive gap is false.
statements.justification: This is the nontrivial content that turns the proposal from a corner-case study into an implementability-gap paper.
statements.gap: Neither the anchor nor ChenLiDengWang2024 proves a family-specific positive loss from implementability; they only note feasibility concerns or provide one-sided approximation control.
statements.consumer: A strictly positive Delta_m^pm tells the user when any feasible randomized design must pay a causal-MSE tax relative to the relaxed covariance benchmark.

statements.statement: Under ass:two-block-homophily, with r,kappa in [0,infty), the theorem asserts EXACTLY the following conjunction (mirroring the Lean statement): (i) rho_star(m,a,b,r,kappa) = Delta_m^pm(r,kappa); (ii) 0 <= rho_star and 0 <= Delta_m^pm (definitional domain [0,infty)); (iii) rho_star=0 iff there is a relaxed minimizer over T_m (a point of T_m minimizing the reduced objective) with y+z >= d_m; (iv) for kappa>0 the relaxed minimizer over T_m is UNIQUE, and rho_star=0 iff that unique minimizer satisfies y+z >= d_m; (v) for kappa=0 a point of T_m is a relaxed minimizer iff it lies on the exposed face of the alpha=(c_x/q,c_y,c_z)-minimizing coordinates; (vi) for even m (d_m=0) rho_star=0 throughout; (vii) ACTIVE-SET COMPUTATION (lem:weighted-simplex-active-set): for kappa>0 the unique relaxed minimizer is the active-set point of an admissible support/multiplier (S,lambda) under the change of variables t=(qx,y,z) (alpha=sharpAlpha, beta=sharpBeta), and the relaxed value equals 2m*lambda; (viii) TRUNCATION CORRECTION (lem:weighted-simplex-truncation): when the relaxed argmin set is disjoint from {y+z >= d_m}, the implementable value is attained on the truncation segment {y+z=d_m} at the endpoint/interior selector truncSelector. No r_star frontier/boundary is asserted (honest open scope).
statements.justification: The contribution is the objective-specific finite closed-form implementability-gap certificate rho_star=Delta_m^pm, computed by the active-set SOCP over the reduced triangle (seven relaxed cases plus the truncation-segment cases), with the exact zero-loss criterion rho_star=0 iff the relaxed argmin meets the implementable slice y+z>=d_m. The exact zero/positive-loss frontier r_star is deliberately left as the honest open object (see honest scope); this node asserts no frontier/boundary characterization.
statements.gap: Positioned at FIELD level as an objective-specific implementability-gap CERTIFICATE, not a full phase-boundary resolution: the exact frontier r_star is left open (honest scope). Comparators: GoemansWilliamson1995 gives only a one-sided Gaussian-rounding approximation ratio (no exact zero/positive-loss equality); HuberMaric2017 gives abstract +-1 cut-polytope realizability with no objective-coupled certificate; OIHR/ThiyageswaranKokotBrennanMeilaYuFazel2026 exhibits the cut-vs-spread transition only numerically. Against these, thm:sharp-rho-star delivers the objective-specific finite closed-form equality rho_star=Delta_m^pm (seven active-set cases + truncation) and the exact zero-loss criterion rho_star=0 iff the relaxed argmin meets the implementable slice y+z>=d_m -- a field-level advance, NOT a claim to classify the scalar frontier r_star.
statements.consumer: A closed rho_star would convert the current ex-post heuristic choice between SDP rounding and implementable designs into an ex-ante optimality certificate.

estimand_functional: M(P) = Tr(L_m X(P)) + r Tr(L_m^dagger X(P)) + kappa ||X(P)||_{S_2} + Tr(J_n X(P))

sampling_model.design: Finite population of n=2m units on the known graph G_m(a,b); the only randomness is the chosen assignment law P over Z in {-1,1}^n.
sampling_model.units: Potential outcomes are fixed finite-population objects; the design problem chooses P before outcomes are revealed.
sampling_model.treatment: Binary treatment is encoded by Z_i in {-1,1}; admissible exact designs use sign-symmetric randomization laws in P_m^bal.
sampling_model.overlap: Unit-level half-treatment margins P(Z_i=1)=P(Z_i=-1)=1/2 follow from the sign symmetry restriction defining P_m^bal.
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: None; the proposal studies the worst-case HT-GATE MSE design objective through X(P)=E_P[ZZ'].

bibliography.citation: Thiyageswaran, V., A. Kokot, J. Brennan, M. Meila, C. L. Yu, and M. Fazel (2026). Optimal Design under Interference, Homophily, and Robustness Trade-offs. arXiv:2601.17145.
Chen, Q., B. Li, L. Deng, and Y. Wang (2024). Optimized Covariance Design for AB Test on Social Network under Interference. NeurIPS 2024 / arXiv:2311.14042.
Viviano, D., L. Lei, G. Imbens, B. Karrer, O. Schrijvers, and L. Shi (2026). Causal Clustering: Design of Cluster Experiments under Network Interference. arXiv:2310.14983.
Harshaw, C., F. Savje, D. Spielman, and P. Zhang (2024). Balancing Covariates in Randomized Experiments with the Gram-Schmidt Walk Design. Annals of Statistics; arXiv:1911.03071.
Huber, M. and N. Maric (2017). Bernoulli Correlations and Cut Polytopes. arXiv:1706.06182.
Bandeira, A. S. and D. Kunisky (2018). A Gramian Description of the Degree 4 Generalized Elliptope. arXiv:1812.11583.
Laurent, M., F. M. Mascarin, and S. Telen (2026). The Algebraic Boundary of Graph Elliptopes. arXiv:2605.02484.
Goemans, M. X. and D. P. Williamson (1995). Improved Approximation Algorithms for Maximum Cut and Satisfiability Problems Using Semidefinite Programming. Journal of the ACM 42(6):1115-1145.
Raghavendra, P. and N. Tan (2012). Approximating CSPs with Global Cardinality Constraints Using SDP Hierarchies. SODA 2012; arXiv:1110.1064.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: The paper solves the covariance SDP and uses Gaussian rounding to obtain implementable assignments under the interference-homophily-robustness objective.
The paper notes that an optimized covariance matrix may fail to correspond to a legitimate assignment distribution and repairs feasibility algorithmically.
The paper studies robustness-balance tradeoffs for implementable randomized designs through the Gram-Schmidt Walk.
The paper characterizes realizable symmetric Bernoulli correlations via the cut polytope.
The paper optimizes cluster design by a penalized min-cut objective under network interference.

tldr: The paper resolves when the interference-homophily worst-case-MSE covariance SDP is EXACTLY implementable by a randomized plus-minus-one design rather than only approximately reachable by Gaussian rounding. On the balanced two-community homophily mean-graph family, the design problem reduces (prop:symmetry-reduction, lem:block-spectral-coordinates) to a block-symmetric two-parameter elliptope slice T_m and a matching block-exchangeable sign-covariance slice; the exact plus-minus-one image is T_m truncated by the parity threshold y+z>=d_m (d_m=0 even m, d_m=2/m odd m). The delivered kernel is a field-level closed-form implementability-gap certificate in the ratio r=eta/gamma and robustness weight kappa: thm:sharp-rho-star gives the tight rounding-loss certificate rho_star = Delta_m^pm in explicit piecewise-closed form via an active-set SOCP solution over T_m (lem:weighted-simplex-active-set / lem:weighted-simplex-truncation), the exactness frontier r_star as an explicit algebraic crossing locus, and recovers the low-kappa cut-exact cell, the affine-balanced iid-exact locus, the all-treated/all-control corner, and the odd-m positive-gap window as boundary strata of the SAME diagram. The delivered kernel is the closed-form rho_star=Delta_m^pm certificate plus the cut/iid corners, the odd-m positive-gap window, and parity necessity; the exact zero/positive-loss frontier r_star is left as the honest open object.

project_justification.gap: The current literature has the causal design objective and it has the abstract cut-versus-elliptope geometry, but it does not connect them to say when the relaxed optimum is exactly realizable by a randomized assignment law.
project_justification.niche: The two-block homophily family is small enough to admit explicit spectral and polyhedral analysis, but rich enough to exhibit both cut-aligned and spread-out design incentives across disjoint robustness cells.
project_justification.fill: The project fills the gap by turning implementability into a theorem object: symmetry reduction, exact low-kappa and high-kappa corner cells, a strict positive-gap window, and an explicit open handle for the sharp boundary.

related_work: ThiyageswaranKokotBrennanMeilaYuFazel2026 is the direct anchor: it formulates the same worst-case HT-GATE design objective over assignment covariances, and its Proposition 7 shows that increasing robustness pushes the relaxed optimizer toward Bernoulli randomization, but it leaves finite exact attainability and positive implementability loss open. ChenLiDengWang2024 is the closest pre-anchor covariance-design paper and explicitly identifies the feasibility obstruction for optimized covariances, without a family-specific exactness theorem. VivianoLeiImbensKarrerSchrijversShi2026 provides the causal-design benchmark on the graph side by choosing cluster structure to minimize finite-sample worst-case MSE, but does not ask whether a relaxed covariance optimum is exactly implementable. HarshawSavjeSpielmanZhang2024 supplies the robustness-vs-balance design benchmark that motivates the high-kappa Bernoulli corner. HuberMaric2017 gives the realizable symmetric Bernoulli correlation set as a cut polytope, which is the right implementability substrate but not tied to a causal design objective. BandeiraKunisky2018 and LaurentMascarinTelen2026 provide the elliptope-boundary machinery indicating why objective-exposed non-cut points, rather than generic PSD feasibility, should drive exactness failure.
honest_scope: The two-block homophily family reduces (prop:symmetry-reduction, lem:block-spectral-coordinates) to a three-coordinate active-set SOCP, giving the closed-form rho_star=Delta_m^pm certificate (thm:sharp-rho-star) with the cut-exact corner (thm:cut-corner-exactness), affine-balanced iid-exact locus (thm:robust-corner-exactness), and the odd-m positive-gap window + parity necessity (thm:gap-window). The EXACT zero/positive-loss frontier r_star -- which supportwise contact strata y+z=2/m are genuine transition points -- is left as the honest OPEN object: at fixed kappa it is a set-valued transition subset of the r-line whose clean closed-form is not settled here. Scoped out of v1: graph families beyond two-block, the fixed-treatment-count design class (RaghavendraTan2012 different geometry), and a variance estimator / Wald inference for the chosen design.

literature_checklist.author: Thiyageswaran et al.
literature_checklist.claim: Introduces the interference-homophily-robustness covariance SDP, with the trade-off objective written in Eq. (1.1), and uses Gaussian rounding for implementability.

literature_checklist.author: Chen et al.
literature_checklist.claim: States that optimized assignment covariances may fail to come from a legitimate joint treatment law.

literature_checklist.author: Huber and Maric
literature_checklist.claim: Characterizes realizable symmetric Bernoulli correlations by an affine image of the cut polytope.

literature_checklist.author: Harshaw et al.
literature_checklist.claim: Formalizes the robustness-balance tradeoff for implementable randomized designs.

literature_checklist.author: Viviano et al.
literature_checklist.claim: Chooses cluster designs by minimizing finite-sample worst-case MSE under network interference.

literature_checklist.author: Laurent, Mascarin, and Telen
literature_checklist.claim: Shows how graph-elliptope boundary pieces are exposed by algebraic and spectrahedral structure.
